item.title: Second edition is here!
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
Clean code in Python, secôÙond ediôÙtion has been pubôÙlished, and here are the main highôÙlightôÙs!

Leer mûÀsãÎ (quedan 2 minutos de lectura)

item.title: Subtleties of Python
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
In our proôÙfesôÙsion, atôÙtenôÙtion to deôÙtail is of utôÙmost imôÙporôÙtance. Any good softôÙware enôÙgiôÙneer unôÙderôÙstands that deôÙtails matôÙter a lot, as they make the difôÙferôÙence beôÙtween a workôÙing unit or a disôÙasôÙter 1.

This is why clean code is not just about forôÙmatôÙting or arôÙrangôÙing the code. It's neiôÙther a foible. It is inôÙstead, payôÙing atôÙtenôÙtion exôÙactôÙly to those deôÙtails that will make a big difôÙferôÙence in proôÙducôÙtion.

Let's see some exôÙamôÙples of this in Python.

Leer mûÀsãÎ (quedan 4 minutos de lectura)

item.title: Notes on PySS18
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
Last weekôÙend an amazôÙing ediôÙtion of Python San SeôÙbastiûÀn took place. Here are the main highôÙlights of what the conôÙferôÙence looked like to me.

Leer mûÀsãÎ (quedan 2 minutos de lectura)

item.title: Exploring Generators and Coroutines
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
Let's reôÙvisôÙit the idea of genôÙerôÙaôÙtors in Python, in orôÙder to unôÙderôÙstand how the supôÙport for corouôÙtines was achieved in latôÙest verôÙsions of Python (3.6, at the time of this writôÙing).

By reôÙviewôÙing the mileôÙstones on genôÙerôÙaôÙtors, chronoôÙlogôÙiôÙcalôÙly, we can get a betôÙter idea of the evoôÙluôÙtion that lead to asynôÙchroôÙnous proôÙgramôÙming in Python.

We will reôÙview the main changes in Python that reôÙlate to genôÙerôÙaôÙtors and asynôÙchroôÙnous proôÙgramôÙming, startôÙing with PEP-255 (SimôÙple GenôÙerôÙaôÙtors), PEP-342 (CorouôÙtines via EnôÙhanced GenôÙerôÙaôÙtors), PEP-380 (SynôÙtax for delôÙeôÙgatôÙing to a SubôÙ-ôÙGenôÙerôÙaôÙtor), and finôÙishôÙing with PEP-525 (AsynôÙchronous GenôÙerôÙaôÙtors).

Leer mûÀsãÎ (quedan 16 minutos de lectura)

item.title: Refuse the temptation to pass
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
This is an opinôÙion I someôÙtimes reôÙmemôÙber when seeôÙing some code. Don't take it as a strong adôÙvice, or a rule, but inôÙstead as a genôÙerôÙal guideôÙline that might help you to imôÙprove the code slightôÙly.

On this post I will share a tiny and opinôÙionôÙatôÙed arôÙguôÙment about why there are usuôÙalôÙly betôÙter things to do in the code inôÙstead of just pass.

Leer mûÀsãÎ (quedan 1 minutos de lectura)

item.title: Discovering Descriptors
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
This talk was preôÙsentôÙed at PyôÙCon CZ 2017 and EuôÙroPyôÙthon 2017. Here are the refôÙerôÙences and reôÙsources for the preôÙsenôÙtaôÙtion.

The code used for the talk, is availôÙable here and conôÙtains some exôÙplaôÙnaôÙtions along with the exôÙamôÙples.
